# Redbarn Wood Turning

> Handcrafted woodwork by Michael Cedoz in Mount Vernon, Ohio. Specializing in hand-turned bowls, custom furniture, wooden pens, and custom commissions using locally sourced Knox County hardwoods (black walnut, white oak, cherry, maple, ash).

Redbarn Wood Turning is a one-person workshop producing heirloom-quality wood pieces built entirely by hand from storm-felled and salvaged Ohio trees. Each piece is unique, shaped on a lathe or at the workbench, and finished with food-safe or hardwax oils depending on use. Pieces typically take 12–18 months from fallen tree to finished work because of air-drying requirements.

## About

- [About Michael Cedoz](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/about.html): The craftsman, his story, and the workshop in Mount Vernon, Ohio.

## Work

- [Gallery](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/gallery.html): Photos of recent and representative pieces — bowls, pens, dining tables, live-edge consoles, custom commissions, and outdoor kitchens.

## Contact & Commissioning

- [Contact Michael](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/contact.html): Email and contact form for custom commissions, pricing, and general inquiries. Email: michael@redbarnwoodturning.com.
- [Events](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/events.html): Upcoming craft fairs and artisan markets, primarily in central Ohio.

## Blog & Process

- [From Fallen Tree to Finished Bowl](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/blog-fallen-tree-to-bowl.html): The full 18-month process of turning a single black walnut into a finished bowl — wet turning, air drying, rough turning vs. finish turning.
- [Why the Wood Matters](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/blog-why-wood-matters.html): How species, grain, and local sourcing shape every piece. Cherry vs. walnut vs. spalted maple.
- [Anatomy of a Build: The Oak Kitchen Table](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/blog-oak-kitchen-table.html): Step-by-step walkthrough of a custom white oak kitchen table build, from slab selection through hardwax oil finish.
- [How to Care for Your Handmade Wood Piece](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/blog-wood-care.html): Maintenance guide for hardwax oil finishes, turned bowls, and pens.
- [What Is Wood Turning, Anyway?](https://redbarnwoodturning.com/blog-what-is-wood-turning.html): An introduction to the craft for people new to the term.

## Reference

- Location: Mount Vernon, Knox County, Ohio, USA
- Founder and sole craftsman: Michael Cedoz
- Materials: Locally sourced hardwoods, primarily black walnut, white oak, red oak, cherry, sugar maple, ash — mostly from storm-felled trees in Knox County and surrounding Ohio counties.
- Typical lead times: Bowls 12–18 months when starting from wet wood; furniture 2–4 months from pre-dried stock.
- Finishes used: Food-safe mineral oil and beeswax for bowls and kitchen items; hardwax oil (Osmo / Rubio Monocoat-style) for furniture; CA (cyanoacrylate) finish for pens.
